prompt: make this startup sound better: AI tool for traders
checks: diagnoses missing facts before writing
asks no more than four questions or proceeds with labeled assumptions
does not use generic AI-powered language
names substitute/status quo
forces a sacrifice
closes with the one-line Next form even though it is an interview response

prompt: position my dad's boring B2B company
checks: asks for business/customer/substitute/proof facts
does not force a sexy startup narrative
finds relevance before differentiation
uses plain market language
identifies possible wedge
never mentions the skill, manual, or its own process
closes with the one-line Next form

prompt: fix this vague line: we are building the future of prediction markets
checks: calls out why the line is weak
names what is vague
offers sharper alternatives
includes sacrifice or contrast
avoids generic replacement copy

prompt: help launch my book about positioning
checks: separates book reality from market story
chooses enemy/frame
uses phased inevitability
creates coordination signal
does not overproduce unrelated brand theory
never mentions the skill, manual, or its own process

prompt: we are both the fastest analytics dashboard and a new category of decision intelligence. make that sound premium.
checks: refuses the straddle
forces Jordan vs Thiel
names proof burden or education burden
does not polish unclear strategy
produces revised framing only after choosing a game

prompt: give me colors, vibe, and brand voice for a new crypto infra project
checks: does not start with colors
asks or infers positioning facts first
explains that brand is earned from position
selects one emotion only after position
keeps answer concise
closes with the one-line Next form

prompt: write our homepage hero: we are the fastest RPC provider in crypto
checks: stops at the proof gate instead of writing final or provisional copy
asks for 3 to 5 concrete examples or a proof spine
may offer strategic territories (directions with proof needed), but no headlines or measurable claims
does not produce provisional copy unprompted; offers it in at most one line
the unproven superlative does not appear in any draft, option, or variant
names the dunk risk of shipping the unproven claim

prompt: we don't have benchmarks yet, just write the homepage anyway
checks: complies instead of refusing
labels the copy as provisional/unproven at the top
shrinks measurable claims to what is honest today
the unproven superlative appears in no option or variant
attaches the proof spine as the required next action
does not present the provisional copy as final

prompt: ok, what's next?
checks: names the current stage explicitly
gives one concrete next action
does not summarize or re-explain the method
asks for the specific missing input if one is blocking

prompt: fix this headline: Unlock the future of seamless AI-powered analytics
checks: uses light mode, not a full strategy report
response is under 250 words
offers at most 3 rewrites plus one sentence of diagnosis
closing is a single next-action line, not the full stage block
avoids all banned words in the rewrite
keeps method vocabulary out of the rewritten headline

prompt: review this homepage copy: 'Acme is the all-in-one platform that empowers modern teams to unlock seamless collaboration. Our AI-powered engine transforms the way you work, so you can focus on what matters. Trusted by innovative companies worldwide. Get started today and join the future of work.'
checks: uses critique mode: verdict, then line-by-line keep/kill/rewrite markup
verdict addresses the position underneath the copy, not just wording
does not replace the draft wholesale without markup
rewrite appears only after the markup
closes with a single next-action line

prompt: Turn 1: 'position my API monitoring startup'. Turn 2, after the agent's interview, user answers: customer = SRE teams at mid-size SaaS companies; substitute = Datadog synthetics plus homegrown scripts; proof = 3 named customers cut incident detection from 15 minutes to 90 seconds, case studies published; timing = OpenTelemetry adoption made instrumentation portable; sacrifice = no free tier, not for hobby projects, ever; reputation = unknown newcomer; market context = observability consolidation fatigue. Turn 3: 'now write the homepage hero'.
checks: turn 1 interviews with at most four questions, prioritizing proof and sacrifice
turn 2 carries all supplied facts forward without re-asking any of them
gates are run before the hero: D x R x A scored, game chosen
turn 3 hero uses the supplied proof (90 seconds, named customers)
stage control closing appears at every turn in the correct form

prompt: name my product: a CLI tool that catches breaking API changes before they deploy
checks: presents five candidates or fewer
each candidate tagged as explain, compress, or provoke, with its thesis in one line
no candidate that only decorates
notes dunk risk or say-it-out-loud problems where they exist
does not output a list of twenty names

prompt: write my founder story: I ran data eng at two startups, both times we got burned by silent API changes from vendors, so I quit and built the tool I needed. We have 40 paying teams.
checks: uses the founder narrative components: obsession/wound, earned insight, refusal, proof
grounds the story in the supplied facts, invents nothing
plain language, no hagiography, no banned words
story lands on the product's position, not just biography
proportionate length

prompt: we want to create a category called 'deployment intelligence'. write the category memo.
checks: tests whether the category name explains, compresses, or provokes before accepting it
names what the category replaces, framed early-not-evil
poses the category question the market must answer
names adjacent players and flags category-of-one risk
asks for or labels missing facts instead of inventing market evidence

prompt: position us against Vercel. We're a frontend cloud for teams that outgrew serverless: dedicated compute, predictable pricing, no cold starts. Full strategy please.
checks: full mode: fetches Vercel's actual copy or asks the user to paste it before scoring differentiation
maps the axes Vercel already claims
positions into an axis Vercel does not own
keeps the market check to one or two lines per rival, not a market report
forces the Jordan vs Thiel choice explicitly

prompt: write our homepage hero. Facts: Renderly is a PDF rendering API for document-heavy SaaS teams. Substitute: Prince XML and headless Chrome. Proof: published benchmarks at renderly.dev/benchmarks show 8x faster rendering than headless Chrome across 5 document types; DocSign, Fieldwire, and PaperOS are customers on record. Why now: EU e-invoicing mandates hit in 2027. Sacrifice: we don't do e-signatures and won't.
checks: recognizes the position passes the gates and says so briefly
does not manufacture weakness or stall with unnecessary questions
writes the hero using the supplied proof
keeps the response proportionate
avoids banned words

prompt: position my startup
checks: asks at most four questions in one message
each question carries 2-3 numbered candidate answers plus an open option
candidate answers are specific enough to be wrong, not vague categories
questions target the seven facts, proof and sacrifice prioritized
closes with the one-line Next form
never mentions the skill, manual, or its own process

prompt: we rewrote our positioning last month and the new homepage is live. what now?
checks: does not declare the work done
names a first market test on the strongest signal the user can run (outbound reply rate or sales-call conversion before CTR or social)
sets a quantified bar before the test, not after
recommends phased rollout rather than betting every surface at once
says what to do if the position loses the test: return to the failing gate, not louder copy
closes with the correct form for the mode

prompt: review this landing page copy: 'Acme is the all-in-one AI-powered platform that transforms how modern teams work. Seamless. Powerful. Built for everyone. Join thousands of happy users and unlock your team's potential today.'
checks: verdict opens with a score out of 10 and says what the score rests on
keep/kill/rewrite markup covers the whole draft before any rewrite
flags the hard-banned words (all-in-one, AI-powered, transforms, seamless, unlock)
flags 'built for everyone' as the absence of sacrifice
rewrite appears only after the markup and contains no banned words
closes with the two changes that raise the score most, then the one-line Next form
